Plan & Activities

14 — 19 September

Got questions?

Anyone between 25 and 35 who gets restless when life gets too comfortable. You don't need to know anyone coming, most people won't. You just need to be the kind of person who says yes first and figures out the details later.

8. Small enough that everyone actually knows each other by day two, big enough that there's always good energy around.

Camping fees, transport throughout the trip, and some activities along the way. Food is not included, we'll do shared meals together but costs are split on the spot.

Simple. €500 includes a tent for the trip. If you already have your own gear and want to bring it, €450 is your price.

We leave from Lisbon on the 14th and finish in Sagres on the 19th. After lunch on the last day we'll be driving back to Lisbon and you're welcome to join. If you'd rather stay in Sagres and keep the feeling going, that's perfectly fine too.

Francisco or Vasco will be behind the wheel. You just have to show up.

In tents, at campsites along the coast. Nothing glamorous. That's the point.

We eat together. Francisco and Vasco will take the lead on cooking and logistics, but this is a group effort and everyone pitches in. Think fire-cooked meals, local markets, and the occasional roadside find.

There's a route and there are campsites. Beyond that, the day shapes itself. Weather, mood, a road nobody noticed on the map — all fair game.

Not at all. There'll be swimming, walking on uneven terrain, and carrying your own bag. If you can handle a beach day with a bit of hiking thrown in, you're good. For surf, no experience needed either — the spots we go to are beginner-friendly and there'll be people in the group of all levels.

We encourage leaving them in your bag during the day. No policy, no enforcement, just a gentle nudge to actually be there.

We'll have a support car on standby for any emergencies. You won't be stranded.

We'll send all payment details by email after your application. No surprises.

Full refund up to 30 days before the trip. After that we can't guarantee it, so travel insurance is a good idea and we recommend it even if it's not required.

Sleeping bag, a headlamp, a light jacket for cool coastal nights, swimwear, reef-safe sunscreen, a reusable water bottle, and clothes you don't mind getting sandy and salty. Leave the suitcase at home, a backpack is all you need. And maybe a book for the quieter moments.

Costa Vicentina · 14–19 September
A minimalist trip across the southwest coast of Portugal.

With tents €500
Without tents €450
